name: Apple Card

earning_rates.notes: 3x on Apple products, apps, subscriptions, and services purchased directly from Apple
2x on all other purchases (except cash back category)

other_benefits.name: Daily Cash
other_benefits.description: Instant cash back (not points) deposited daily to your Apple Cash card

other_benefits.name: No Fees
other_benefits.description: No annual fees, no late fees, no foreign transaction fees, no penalty APR

other_benefits.name: Apple Pay Integration
other_benefits.description: Seamless integration with Apple Pay for secure mobile payments

other_benefits.name: Titanium Card
other_benefits.description: Physical titanium credit card with no card number embossed (for use where Apple Pay is not accepted)

sources.notes: JPMorgan Chase official release: Chase to become new Apple Card issuer; transition expected in approximately 24 months.
Goldman Sachs official release confirming agreement to transition Apple Card program to Chase.

_no_credits_note: Apple Card ($0 AF) — no fixed-USD recurring credits. Daily Cash is an earning rate (1-3%, not a credit). Booking.com 2% travel credits = earning rate. Uber One 6-mo trial = one-time promo. Hertz benefits = qualitative. Previous entries 'Daily Cash' and 'Apple Card Family' were category-confused (earning rate / family feature, not recurring credits). Removed 2026-04-27.
_card_status_alert: Apple and JPMorgan Chase announced Chase will become the new issuer of Apple Card, with an expected transition in approximately 24 months from the Jan 2026 announcement. Keep issuer as Goldman Sachs until the account/card program transition is actually complete.
